subject: Add Spike microservice chart
commit_message:
Add Spike microservice chart

This is the Helm chart for the newly open-sourced Spike microservice.

Change-Id: I4bba11b87db0df22d7beedc528a4dea738af5378
Issue-ID: AAI-1524
Signed-off-by: Michael Arrastia <MArrasti@amdocs.com>

Please note that the model/edge_props will probably require moving up to a common area as Gizmo is also including these as resources and therefore they are duplicated.

Patch Set 2:

(7 comments)

> Uploaded patch set 2.

Most comments addressed (thank you Borislav).
However, the nodePort is still set to 77. It appears there are no further ports available in the 302 range. Would be grateful for any advice on how to resolve this.

Patch Set 3:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/225/

Patch Set 3:

> Uploaded patch set 3.

This is to update the nodePort. Notice this changes the range via the nodePortPrefix in values.yaml to 304 as 302 is exhausted. Together with this there is an update to service.yaml which I am not entirely sure about.  Would be grateful for any comments.

Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/225/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/225

Patch Set 2:

> > Uploaded patch set 3.
 > 
 > This is to update the nodePort. Notice this changes the range via
 > the nodePortPrefix in values.yaml to 304 as 302 is exhausted.
 > Together with this there is an update to service.yaml which I am
 > not entirely sure about.  Would be grateful for any comments.

This is a bit problematic, because if global.nodePortPrefix will be overwritten (using command line for example) the chart nodePortPrefix will remain.
Currently there is a discussion in oom team regarding what to do with nodePorts. Hopefully it will be resolved soon. I do not see port 77 used except in the wiki. Maybe it would be better to ask in the distribution list oof team whether they need this port and if not, use port 30277 (and update it in the wiki).
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4: -Verified

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/269/

Patch Set 4:

> Uploaded patch set 4.

- Correct nodePortPrefix assignment in service.yaml and remove fixed range allocation in values.yaml. This reverts Spike to the 302 nodePort range.
- Currently pursuing possible use of port 77, have sent request to OOF team to see if they can spare that port.
Thank you Borislav for your comments and advice.

Patch Set 6:

The target message router for Casablanca is DMaaP. I need to patch the spike-beans.xml to use DMaaP instead of Kafka. Please don't approve yet.

Patch Set 6:

@Michael, if you don't want this merged, then it would be best for you to self score it a -2...that way others won't be able to merge until clear it.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

I have to patch the spike-beans.xml configuration to use DMaaP instead of Kafka.  Please don't approve until this is done.
Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 7:

> Uploaded patch set 7.

Updated spike-beans.xml to configure event consumer/publisher to use DMaaP (instead of Kafka).
